title: Setting automations against mirrored columns

posts.author: taylor.huber (Taylor Huber)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

If i change a status in the main board I would like it to change in the mirrored board and then set up an automation in the mirrored board for the taks to change groups based on the status selection.

What are you trying to achieve

Our team works in 2 types boards : a main board (internal to-dos or client boards) and then a mirrored board (that shows department specific tasks from each of the main boards). I would like the mirrored board to be able to do more in terms of organizing tasks so that it doesn’t look as overwhelming & try to acheive a better workflow for everyone.

I want them to be able to put / update something in the main board, but then be able to see those tasks in an organized fashion in their department specific board. These department boards helps everyone see what tasks, specific to their team, are still open, done, etc. I can’t have them update things in more than one spot because they won’t do it (and currently are not). Right now, it’s just a huge list of to-dos that are unorganized because they aren’t being filed away in an automated manner.

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can do this with 2+ additional automations (or 1-2 workflows, but I’ll stick with automations for now). If you have Enterprise, let me know and I’ll show you how to do it with workflows.

First, create another status column that is the same as the mirror column. Then use the Mirror Automation. Then create automations that move the item to the group based on the new status selection.
